channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Body of missing grandmother who fell into sinkhole recovered after 4-day search
item.description: The search for 64-year-old Elizabeth Pollard, who fell into a sinkhole in Pittsburgh, has concluded after the coroner's office confirmed with news outlets that her body had been found.
item.dc:creator: Amber Baker, KREX 5

item.title: USDA requires bulk testing of raw milk supply for bird flu virus
item.description: The nation’s milk supply must be tested for the bird flu virus H5N1 under a new order announced Friday by the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) – here's why.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KREX 5

item.title: Vance tells residents in hurricane-stricken North Carolina that they haven't been forgotten
item.description: FAIRVIEW, N.C. (AP) — Vice President-elect JD Vance on Friday assured residents of western North Carolina still cleaning up from Hurricane Helene that they haven't been forgotten as he surveyed storm wreckage and talked to first responders in one of his first public appearances since the election.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and GARY ROBERTSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Indianapolis police officers are acquitted in the 2022 death of a man at his parents’ home
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Two Indianapolis police officers were acquitted of manslaughter and other charges Friday in the death of a man after officers shocked him with a Taser and restrained him face down while handcuffing him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled nominee to lead the Pentagon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Crews recover the body of a woman from a Pennsylvania sinkhole after a 4-day search
item.description: The remains of a woman who fell into a sinkhole were recovered Friday, four days after she went missing while searching for her cat, a state police spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Middle East latest: Lebanon closes all its land border crossings with Syria except one
item.description: Lebanon is closing all its land border crossings with Syria except for a main one that links Beirut with the Syrian capital Damascus, the General Security Directorate said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump taps forceful ally of hard-line immigration policies to head Customs and Border Protection
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The picture of who will be in charge of executing President-elect Donald Trump's hard-line immigration and border policies has come into sharper focus after he announced his picks to head Customs and Border Protection and also the agency tasked with deporting immigrants in the coun
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Millions will lose health coverage if ACA subsidies expire: CBO
item.description: If Affordable Care Act subsidies are allowed to expire at the end of 2025 millions of people will lose their insurance and premiums will jump, according to a Congressional Budget Office report.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court will decide if Palestinian authorities can be sued in US over attacks in Middle East
item.description: The Supreme Court has agreed to settle a years-long legal dispute over whether Palestinian authorities can be sued in U.S. courts by Americans killed or wounded in terrorism attacks in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Officials release bodycam video of police shooting that killed 15-year-old Ohio boy
item.description: The mayor and police chief in an Ohio city where a 15-year-old boy was fatally shot by police on Thanksgiving night said the bodycam video raises questions that must be addressed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge urges NYC subway chokehold jury to keep deliberating after it struggles to reach verdict
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The judge in Daniel Penny’s subway chokehold manslaughter trial urged jurors on Friday to continue their deliberations after they told him they couldn't reach a unanimous vote on the top charge.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge upholds Naval Academy's race-conscious admissions policy
item.description: A federal judge upheld the Naval Academy’s race-conscious admissions policy Friday, rejecting a challenge from the group that succeeded at the Supreme Court in gutting affirmative action at civilian higher education institutions.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: 186 minors cited in Utah bar sting
item.description: The investigation was reportedly sparked by multiple DUI arrests linked to the bar earlier in the year.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation

item.title: Photos show face of 'person of interest' wanted in shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: The NYPD released the photos amid a city-wide manhunt for the gunman who shot UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson on Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KTLA 5

item.title: What to know about the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The masked gunman who killed the leader of one of the largest U.S. health insurance companies in front of Manhattan surveillance cameras remained at large Friday as the nation’s largest police department hunted for him. UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, 50, died in the ambush early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tyre Sampson's family awarded $310 million after fatal fall on Florida ride
item.description: Tyre Sampson, 14, was tragically killed after falling to his death off a freefall tower drop ride at Orlando’s ICON Park.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Company fined $172K after children employed to clean Iowa pork plant
item.description: They employed 11 children to "use corrosive cleaners to clean head splitters, jaw pullers, bandsaws, neck clippers and other equipment.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, WGN 9

item.title: Killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO uncorks anger at insurance industry
item.description: The killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson on a Manhattan street is the latest moment to shine a light on the tensions roiling American life.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: Splash pads associated with waterborne disease outbreaks: CDC
item.description: "Children are at increased risk for exposure to pathogens in contaminated splash pad water," the CDC said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Anthony Salisbury as deputy Homeland Security advisor
item.description: Anthony Salisbury is currently a special agent in charge of Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) in Miami.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Police believe gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare CEO has left New York City
item.description: Police officials believe the gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer may have fled the city on a bus.
item.dc:creator: Michael Balsamo, Jake Offenhartz And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New clues emerge as investigators hunt for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New clues emerged Thursday in the hunt for the masked gunman who stalked and killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, including possible leads about his travel before the shooting and a message scrawled on ammunition found at the crime scene.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yes, Chipotle is raising menu prices again: Here's why
item.description: The cost of your burrito bowl is going up. Chipotle has raised menu prices for the first time in over a year.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey leaders speak to DHS as unusual drone sightings now also reported over New York
item.description: New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy spoke with federal and state officials about the sightings of drones in recent weeks, but said there was no threat to the public.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video appears to show UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's killer walking toward hotel before shooting
item.description: A man that matches the description of the suspect wanted for savagely murdering UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson was captured on video walking toward where the shooting happened.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An Ole Miss student exchanged messages with the man now on trial in his killing, police say
item.description: In the final hours before University of Mississippi student Jimmy “Jay” Lee disappeared, sexually explicit Snapchat messages were exchanged between his account and the account of the man now on trial in his killing, an investigator testified Thursday. Sheldon “Timothy” Herrington Jr.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Father of victim in NYC subway chokehold case sues defendant Daniel Penny
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The father of the victim at the center of the fatal New York City subway chokehold trial has sued the defendant as a Manhattan jury continued to deliberate the case Thursday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NASA's stuck astronauts hit 6 months in space. Just 2 more to go
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Known across the globe as the stuck astronauts, Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams hit the six-month mark in space Thursday with two more to go.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Things to know about the federal investigation into the Memphis Police Department
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A U.S. Department of Justice investigation found that the Memphis Police Department committed a host of civil rights violations, including using excessive force, making illegal traffic stops and disproportionately targeting Black people in the majority Black city.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Link to adult website on Mattel's 'Wicked' dolls at center of proposed class action lawsuit
item.description: A mother claims she and her daughter visited the link on the box, only to be "horrified" by what they saw, according to the lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: QVC recalls 1.1 million oven gloves after more than 90 burn injury reports
item.description: QVC is recalling more than 1 million pairs of oven gloves after dozens of people were burned while using them, the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KRON4
